mnemonics.title: PRNG Algorithm Components
mnemonics.content: S.T.O. - State Transition Output. Remember: 'State To Output' - the State gets Transitioned to produce Output.

mnemonics.title: Cryptographic PRNG Properties
mnemonics.content: U-F-B: Unpredictable, Forward secure, Backward secure. Think: 'Unpredictable Future Becomes Secure'.

mnemonics.title: PRNG vs TRNG
mnemonics.content: P.T. - Pseudorandom is Predictable/Reproducible, True random is Unpredictable/Non-reproducible. Remember: 'P' for Pseudorandom = Predictable.

mnemonics.title: PRNG Security Essentials
mnemonics.content: S.E.E.R - Seed must have Entropy, Expose no state, Ensure unpredictability, Reseed periodically.

mnemonics.title: Three Phases of PRNG
mnemonics.content: S.O.R. - Seeding, Operation, Reseeding. Remember: 'Start, Operate, Re-entropy'.

acronyms.usage: Algorithm that produces deterministic sequences from seed appearing random
Hardware-based generator using physical phenomena for randomness
PRNG designed with security properties for cryptographic applications
Common PRNG algorithm using linear recurrence: X(n+1) = (a*X(n) + c) mod m
Cryptographic PRNG based on quadratic residue hardness assumption

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a feedback loop: Seed → Initial State → [Output Function → Output] AND [State Transition Function → New State] → loop back to New State. Think of it as a circular assembly line where the 'work' (state) gets passed along with each 'product' (output) being taken off.
Think of PRNG state as a combination lock with n bits. Maximum period is like trying all possible combinations before finding a repeat. With n bits, at most 2^n unique states exist.
Imagine aPRNG as a shuffled deck of cards. Even knowing previous cards (outputs), predicting the next card (output) is computationally hard - you'd need to track all remaining possibilities.

analogies.analogy: Think of the seed as the 'starting point' in a maze. The PRNG algorithm is like walking through the maze - from the same start point (seed), you'll always take the same path and see the same sights (sequence).
State transition is like the 'next page' rule in a book - each page (state) tells you which page to read next (next state), creating a predictable journey through the book.
Seed expansion is like a recipe - a small amount of yeast (seed with limited entropy) makes a large loaf of bread (long pseudorandom sequence) through the baking process (algorithm).
Statistical PRNG is like a magician's trick that looks random but follows a pattern. Cryptographic PRNG is like a lock - even knowing how it works, you can't open it without the key (seed/state).
